title: Internal Error in `V3EmitC.cpp` related to a literal for an array of strings
body:
To reproduce:
```
git clone https://github.com/jeras/rp32.git -b 0f4b136
cd rp32
cd sim
make -f Makefile.verilator lint
```
The output is:
```
/home/ijeras/VLSI/verilator/bin/verilator --lint-only ../rtl/riscv_isa_pkg.sv ../rtl/rp_br.sv ../rtl/rp_gpr.sv ../rtl/rp_alu.sv ../rtl/rp_core.sv ../tbn/riscv_asm_pkg.sv ../tbn/mem.sv ../tbn/rp_tb.sv --top rp_tb
%Error: Internal Error: ../tbn/riscv_asm_pkg.sv:8:34: ../V3EmitC.cpp:1268: Unknown node type reached emitter: INITITEM
    8 | localparam string REG_X [0:31] = '{"zero", "ra", "sp", "gp", "tp", "t0", "t1", "t2", "s0/fp", "s1", "a0", "a1", "a2", "a3", "a4", "a5",
      |                                  ^~
                        ... See the manual and https://verilator.org for more assistance.
make: *** [Makefile.verilator:98: lint] Error 1
```

Tested using Verilator `stable` and the almost latest commit `f579e557`.

I tried to create a short test to trigger this issue, but I was not able to, the simple test did not trigger a warning, and it run the example with proper printouts as expected. the example tarball is attached.
[test.zip](https://github.com/verilator/verilator/files/6329534/test.zip)
From this I suppose the problem is not in the C emitter itself.

Sorry I cant help fixing this, I would spend days before I could even read C++ code with confidence.
